task_title: KOZO First Governed Runtime Capability v0.8.0
preconditions: QEMU serial smoke and stack initialization evidence are proven
v0.7.4 memory initialization evidence is accepted by the CI validator gate; manual artifact inspection was not completed
v0.7.45 runtime progression entry and runtime initialization evidence are accepted by hosted CI
v0.7.5 controlled runtime loop evidence is accepted by hosted CI run 30057826315
The reconciled runtime progression graph is acyclic and permits first capability evidence only after controlled loop evidence
Odin, pinned Rust, Python, jq, and repository verification tooling are installed
assumptions: contracts/runtime_progression_stages.v0.json is the sole authority for canonical stage order and allowed transitions
Stack, memory, progression-entry, runtime-initialization, and controlled-loop evidence remain proven while first capability evidence requires new CI evidence
KOZO_FIRST_CAPABILITY_OK requires a validated request, exact dispatch, validated response, and KOZO_RUNTIME_RETURN_OK still requires exact status zero

This phase changes only the bounded internal runtime capability, fixed marker bridges, evidence validation, progression status, and documentation; public ABI, syscalls, linker layout, and terminal halt authority remain unchanged

expected_outputs: The verification script is reproducible on a clean tree and emits the current verify artifact
The kernel builds as a binary and as an object artifact without relying on broken default serial inline assembly
The Rust service crosses the assembly bridge through extern syscall_entry instead of a local stub
The harness records protocol_contract_alignment, runtime_trap_path, and execution_proof results that match the current runtime-trap implementation
README.md and ADR-0016 share the canonical wording for the function-call trap path and the workspace ignores generated __pycache__ content
Tracked Python cache artifacts are removed from version control so ignored cache files no longer dirty the workspace
Rust explicitly validates returned payload mutations after syscall_entry and the verify artifact records return_path_proof
Focused return_path_proof tests cover status_bits negative paths and unrelated status_bits text
bridge_alignment validates the ordered live syscall_entry block and rejects dead or unordered bridge snippets
runtime_trap_path validates the live Rust heartbeat request path with focused positive and negative tests
execution_proof validates the live Odin heartbeat branch and serial observations with focused positive and negative tests
The regenerated verification artifact records the hardened bridge, runtime, return-path, and execution proofs
validator_coverage prevents registered validators from entering the harness without focused behavioral negative-path tests
validator_coverage requires declared failure-mode markers to map to behavioral negative tests
ABI manifest v0 provides the Python-side source of truth for proven syscall constants, status constants, heartbeat payload layout, and heartbeat sentinels
Syscall boundary contract v0 describes and validates only the currently proven x86_64 heartbeat trap boundary
syscall_boundary_conformance proves live assembly, Rust, and Odin sources conform to syscall boundary contract v0
Syscall table contract v0 describes and validates the currently proven heartbeat/debug dispatcher branch and unknown-syscall default behavior
syscall_table_conformance proves the live Odin dispatcher implements syscall table contract v0 without uncontracted payload-mutating branches
K_SYSCALL_NOP is governed as a first-class no-payload syscall table entry that returns K_OK and never mutates or inspects heartbeat payload state
The Rust core service exercises K_SYSCALL_NOP through syscall_entry with a null payload pointer and validates the K_OK return status
Syscall class contract v0 classifies only currently proven syscall shapes and validates syscall table class assignments
K_SYSCALL_STATUS is governed as a no-payload status syscall with a null payload argument, K_OK return status, no payload mutation, live Odin dispatch, and a Rust bridge probe
Syscall catalog v0 summarizes nop, status, and debug_heartbeat without owning ABI constants, table structure, class semantics, or runtime behavior
docs/generated/syscall_surface.md provides a deterministic non-authoritative Markdown report generated from the syscall catalog and existing contracts
docs/generated/abi_surface.md provides a deterministic non-authoritative Markdown report generated from the ABI manifest

Governance documents split authority across precedence, invariants, architecture, contracts, coding style, validation, generated artifacts, compatibility, security model, and ADR policy without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, or compatibility behavior
Rust verification uses pinned cargo and rustc version 1.96.0
Release planning documents define phase sequencing, roadmap direction, and release evidence requirements without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, or compatibility behavior
Release governance baseline execution defines release checklist and required checks policy without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, or compatibility behavior
Runtime smoke evidence baseline records a bounded runtime-adjacent object and symbol evidence artifact without changing ABI, syscall, compatibility, or production-readiness claims
Runtime smoke evidence packaging records deterministic metadata and release bundle guidance without adding QEMU boot, hardware trap, compatibility, userspace, or production-readiness claims

Documentation standard v1 governs documentation clarity, audience separation, claim discipline, terminology consistency, onboarding structure, and documentation review without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, validator, compatibility, or production-readiness claims

Documentation consistency corrections keep coding-style authority references and generated governance-index artifact ownership aligned with docs/GOVERNANCE.md and governed report generation without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, validator, compatibility, or production-readiness claims

Runtime evidence review gate documents and validates the runtime-adjacent-object-symbol-smoke evidence type, live evidence paths, release bundle paths, validator ownership, release references, metadata non-goals, and overclaim blockers without adding QEMU boot, hardware trap, compatibility, userspace, or production-readiness claims

CI/runtime evidence policy alignment documents that full CI requires runtime smoke through scripts/verify.sh, release review requires runtime smoke log and metadata, lint remains static unless it runs full verification, and CI uploads runtime smoke artifacts without adding QEMU boot, hardware trap, compatibility, userspace, or production-readiness claims
Bootable runtime baseline records a verified v0.3.0 blocker report naming missing boot protocol, linker script, loader configuration, and boot image packaging instead of claiming QEMU boot evidence

Boot protocol selection records Limine as the initial x86_64 boot protocol and keeps missing_boot_protocol_and_image_packaging active until linker script, loader configuration, image packaging, and QEMU smoke execution exist

Boot image skeleton adds linker script, Limine config, boot image staging, a kernel ELF build path, and validation while reducing the active blocker to missing_qemu_execution_evidence without claiming QEMU boot
QEMU serial smoke attempt adds a fail-closed QEMU command and refines the active blocker to missing_bootable_iso_packaging without claiming QEMU boot or serial evidence

Bootable ISO packaging attempt generates deterministic package metadata and narrows the active blocker to missing_bootable_iso_generation without claiming a bootable ISO, QEMU boot, or serial evidence
Bootable ISO generation path attempts Limine/xorriso packaging, records missing_iso_generation_tooling when local tooling is unavailable, and preserves no-QEMU-boot and no-serial-success claims

CI ISO tooling install adds pinned Limine source acquisition, xorriso installation, CI boot image build attempts, and artifact upload coverage while preserving no-QEMU-boot and no-serial-success claims

QEMU serial smoke evidence adds deterministic QEMU smoke metadata, qemu_smoke_evidence validation, CI QEMU smoke attempts, and the kernel-emitted KOZO_BOOT_SMOKE_OK marker while preserving no-QEMU-boot claims unless serial evidence validates
QEMU timeout blocker handling records the CI-observed qemu_timeout state, adds QEMU stderr evidence, and rejects blocked metadata if the kernel marker is already present

Kernel entry reachability diagnostics add documented Limine serial output, early KOZO markers, v0.4.0 QEMU metadata, and exact blocker taxonomy without claiming QEMU boot unless the boot smoke marker is captured
Limine kernel load path fix uses /boot/kozo/kozo-kernel.elf and classifies Limine executable-open failures as kernel_not_loaded without claiming QEMU boot
Limine ISO kernel load semantics now use boot():/boot/kozo/kozo-kernel.elf, record normalized ISO path visibility metadata, and keep kernel_not_loaded active until CI serial evidence proves otherwise

docs/PHASEMAP.md and docs/ROADMAP.md own release phase sequencing and roadmap direction from the governance document directory without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, boot behavior, or compatibility claims

v0.4.5 classifies Limine lower-half PHDR rejection as limine_lower_half_phdr in kernel ELF reports, QEMU smoke metadata, boot blocker reporting, focused tests, and boot evidence docs while preserving no-QEMU-boot and non-compatibility claim boundaries
v0.4.6 adds docs/CODEBASE_AUDIT.md, updates phase and roadmap sequencing so v0.4.7 is the higher-half linker/entry transition, and records that no cleanup was applied during the audit phase

v0.5.1 aligns governance, planning, audit, release, and evidence documents with the actual v0.5.0 outcome: local verification passes, the pushed CI run for 14fb015 failed in scripts/verify.sh, QEMU serial smoke evidence is not promoted, and the next runtime phase is CI smoke evidence triage.

v0.5.2 CI evidence access hardening prints verification, QEMU smoke, serial/stderr, and boot blocker summaries into full CI logs with if: always(), keeps artifact upload enabled, and avoids requiring gh or authenticated artifact download for first-level triage.

v0.7.1 adds memory_initialization_evidence_contract as the planning authority for future MEMORY_INITIALIZATION_EVIDENCE, reserves KOZO_MEMORY_INIT_OK without emitting it, aligns progression contracts, and preserves runtime behavior.
v0.7.2 reconciles the authoritative progression order, removes the stack-entry-memory cycle, adds deterministic graph-level validation, aligns planning state, and preserves runtime behavior.

v0.7.3 hardens the planned memory evidence contract with exact region geometry, ownership, zero-fill semantics, a bounded survival probe, and pre-halt marker placement without changing runtime behavior.

v0.7.4 implements the controlled static memory region, explicit full-region zero fill, bounded sentinel probe and restoration, KOZO_MEMORY_INIT_OK evidence, and unchanged halt path without adding paging, allocation, runtime progression, compatibility, or production claims.

v0.7.45 implements a fixed internal assembly-to-Odin boundary, validated bootstrap context, bounded static-state probe, Odin-dependent initialization marker, exact return status, and terminal halt continuation without expanding the public ABI or runtime claims.

v0.7.5 implements a bounded three-iteration Odin loop with volatile boot-owned state, ordered iteration evidence, linked backward-edge proof, exact return status, and the unchanged terminal halt continuation without adding scheduler, userspace, compatibility, or production claims.

v0.8.0 implements one versioned internal runtime status request with explicit field validation, one direct dispatcher, one deterministic response handler, response validation, governed capability evidence, and the unchanged return-to-halt path without adding userspace, isolation, scheduler, allocator, interrupt, ABI, syscall, compatibility, or production claims.

cargo check; cargo deny check; cargo audit; cargo nextest run; taplo fmt --check .; taplo check

invariants: The default kernel build path must succeed without broken serial inline assembly
The Rust heartbeat request must cross the assembly bridge through extern syscall_entry rather than a local stub
The verify artifact is only authoritative when the current tree reproduces it via scripts/verify.sh
runtime evidence taxonomy is authoritative for marker and blocker vocabulary; generated smoke metadata remains evidence and does not define taxonomy authority.
Runtime progression stages are authoritative stage governance; the graph remains acyclic and monotonic, and the halt loop remains authoritative after the bounded progression call.
Hosted CI run 29459278491 captured the complete ordered runtime marker sequence and passed runtime_progression_evidence.
expected_behavior: verify.sh succeeds on a clean tree and prints the generated artifact JSON before VERIFY: PASS
odin build kernel succeeds with the default source tree
odin build kernel -build-mode:obj -out:artifacts/kernel.o succeeds and produces a host-build object artifact
verify.sh emits runtime_trap_path: pass when Rust declares and calls extern syscall_entry
Reintroducing the local Rust stub or removing the extern bridge call causes runtime_trap_path to fail
Changing the expected Rust sequence to the wrong value causes return_path_proof to fail
Removing the Rust timestamp check causes return_path_proof to fail
Removing the Rust status_bits check causes return_path_proof to fail
Removing the Odin status_bits success write causes return_path_proof to fail
Moving required bridge snippets outside the live syscall_entry block causes bridge_alignment to fail
Reordering required live syscall_entry anchors causes bridge_alignment to fail
Moving required Rust bridge snippets outside the live helper causes runtime_trap_path to fail
Changing heartbeat request sentinels or ordering causes runtime_trap_path to fail
Moving required Odin mutations outside the live heartbeat branch causes execution_proof to fail
Removing nil guards, status_bits mutation, or serial observations causes execution_proof to fail
A registered validator without a focused test file causes validator_coverage to fail
A placeholder negative-looking test without validator invocation or failure assertion causes validator_coverage to fail
Missing KOZO_NEGATIVE_COVERAGE metadata causes validator_coverage to fail
Missing required markers, unknown markers, missing mapped tests, and non-behavioral mapped tests cause validator_coverage to fail
Removing ABI surface report constants, binding paths, layout fields, sentinels, or generated content causes abi_surface_report to fail
Governance document updates remain bounded to documentation authority, changelog state, and task scope
Verification fails if cargo or rustc do not report pinned version 1.96.0

Release planning documents preserve non-goals for Linux compatibility, POSIX completeness, general userspace execution, process model behavior, VFS behavior, scheduler behavior, ELF loading, file descriptor behavior, and production readiness

Release checklist and required checks policy preserve non-goals for Linux compatibility, POSIX completeness, general userspace execution, process model behavior, VFS behavior, scheduler behavior, ELF loading, file descriptor behavior, and production readiness

Runtime smoke evidence preserves non-goals for Linux compatibility, POSIX completeness, general userspace execution, process model behavior, VFS behavior, scheduler behavior, ELF loading, file descriptor behavior, QEMU boot execution, hardware trap execution, and production readiness

Runtime smoke metadata preserves explicit non-goals for QEMU boot, hardware trap execution, Linux compatibility, userspace execution, process model, VFS behavior, scheduler maturity, ELF loading, file descriptor behavior, and production readiness

Documentation standard changes remain bounded to documentation construction governance, governance precedence, changelog state, task scope, generated governance index refresh, and verification artifact refresh

Documentation consistency fixes remove duplicated precedence drift and replace governance-index placeholder generator wording without altering document authority, runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, or generated report authority

runtime_evidence_review fails if runtime evidence review documentation, release evidence references, release checklist gates, required checks references, or metadata non-goals are missing or drift from the current runtime-adjacent evidence scope

CI/runtime evidence policy alignment preserves the runtime-adjacent-object-symbol-smoke scope while making full CI, lint, required checks, release checklist, and release evidence policy agree on runtime smoke evidence requirements
boot_blocker_report validates that v0.3.0 cannot honestly claim QEMU boot until missing boot protocol and image packaging components are implemented
boot_protocol_decision validates the accepted Limine boot protocol decision, alternatives, non-goals, active blocker wording, and v0.3.2 Boot Image Skeleton references
boot_image_skeleton validates linker script, Limine config, build script, boot image docs, memory support symbols, and blocker state without requiring QEMU execution

boot_tooling validates the Limine and xorriso tooling acquisition policy, local and CI install path documentation, provenance requirements, and narrowed boot blocker state without requiring ISO generation
boot_image_packaging validates v0.3.6 package metadata for either a real packaged ISO or a blocked missing_iso_generation_tooling result without requiring QEMU execution
boot_image_packaging validates v0.4.4 Limine configured-kernel-path semantics, normalized ISO path metadata, and packaged ISO path visibility without claiming QEMU boot
qemu_smoke_evidence validates v0.3.9 QEMU smoke metadata for either a passing serial marker capture or an exact blocked outcome without requiring broad runtime or compatibility claims
qemu_smoke_evidence rejects blocked QEMU metadata if artifacts/runtime/qemu_smoke.log already contains KOZO_BOOT_SMOKE_OK
qemu_smoke_evidence validates v0.4.0 QEMU smoke metadata with early marker, observed marker, timeout, and byte-count diagnostics
QEMU smoke metadata narrows missing Limine or KOZO output to limine_not_reached rather than a broad qemu_timeout blocker
Blocked QEMU metadata fails if the observed marker list does not match the serial and stderr logs
QEMU smoke evidence treats Limine executable-open failures as kernel_not_loaded and preserves no-boot claims unless KOZO_BOOT_SMOKE_OK is captured

kernel_loadability validates v0.4.2 kernel ELF loadability evidence for x86_64 executable format, entry point, _start alignment, program headers, PT_LOAD segments, blocker alignment, and non-claim boundaries

host_dependency_portability validates v0.4.3 host portability policy, rejects user-specific absolute paths and Apple-specific toolchain assumptions, verifies CI/Linux tooling declarations, and preserves local macOS as a convenience path only
All active release planning references resolve to docs/PHASEMAP.md and docs/ROADMAP.md, with historical changelog references preserved as history

kernel_loadability validates v0.4.5 Limine ELF load-layout evidence by requiring PT_LOAD virtual-address metadata, lower-half segment detection, and limine_lower_half_phdr blocker alignment without claiming QEMU boot or kernel entry

Codebase structural audit records stale/dead/brittle/god-file risks, duplicated boot taxonomy, boundary issues, and higher-half transition hazards without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, linker layout, QEMU boot claims, or compatibility claims

v0.4.95 code quality and style audit records duplicated boot marker taxonomy, duplicated blocker taxonomy, QEMU script size, validator coverage size, fixture duplication, and v0.5.0 marker-emission risks without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, linker layout, QEMU marker semantics, or compatibility claims

v0.4.96 QEMU smoke evidence summary generation writes artifacts/runtime/qemu_smoke.summary.txt from existing metadata, logs, and blocker evidence so CI artifacts are easier to inspect without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, linker layout, QEMU marker semantics, or compatibility claims
v0.5.0 emits KOZO_BOOT_SMOKE_OK from the proven assembly serial path after KOZO_EARLY_2_SERIAL_INIT_OK and requires QEMU smoke validation to observe the full ordered marker sequence before passing
v0.6.0 governs post-smoke terminal behavior after KOZO_BOOT_SMOKE_OK without changing ABI or syscall semantics.
runtime_progression_contract defines future halt-to-runtime transition governance without changing runtime behavior or weakening the runtime halt contract.
runtime_progression_entry_contract governs the bounded internal assembly-to-Odin path without overriding terminal halt authority or creating a userspace ABI.
v0.6.4 removes only proven-unused structural debris and documents deferred cleanup without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, linker, marker, halt, or progression contract behavior.

v0.6.5 centralizes QEMU serial smoke marker order, expected marker, outcomes, and blocker vocabulary in a governed runtime evidence taxonomy contract without changing runtime behavior or smoke pass criteria.

v0.6.6 centralizes future runtime progression stage definitions without changing runtime behavior, halt behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, linker layout, QEMU smoke behavior, compatibility claims, or production-readiness claims.

v0.7.2 requires an acyclic progression graph, earlier mandatory prerequisites, monotonic proven status, forward-only transitions, known authorities, and one owner per transition without changing runtime behavior.

v0.7.3 requires exact future region geometry and ownership, full-region zero fill, a bounded sentinel write/read/compare/restore probe, and marker placement before the unchanged halt loop while memory evidence remains planned.
actual_behavior: verify.sh handles an empty changed-files set without aborting and prints the generated verify artifact.
The kernel builds successfully as both the default binary target and the object artifact target.
core_service declares and calls extern syscall_entry instead of a local heartbeat stub.
The verify artifact reports protocol_contract_alignment and runtime_trap_path for the current runtime-trap implementation.
README.md and ADR-0016 use the same canonical statement for the function-call trap path, and __pycache__/ is ignored.
Tracked Python cache artifacts are removed from version control so the remaining __pycache__ files are ignored.
core_service validates the returned status, sequence, timestamp, and status_bits after syscall_entry and the verify artifact reports return_path_proof.
Changing the expected Rust sequence to 0xBADFEED causes return_path_proof to fail.
Removing the Rust timestamp check causes return_path_proof to fail.
Removing the Rust status_bits check causes return_path_proof to fail with a named missing-anchor diagnostic.
Removing the Odin status_bits success write causes return_path_proof to fail with a named missing-anchor diagnostic.
A stray status_bits mention does not satisfy the required Rust return-path status_bits check.
bridge_alignment rejects dead snippets outside syscall_entry with a named diagnostic.
bridge_alignment rejects out-of-order live syscall_entry anchors with a named diagnostic.
bridge_alignment rejects missing dispatcher handoff and missing Odin dispatcher signatures.
runtime_trap_path rejects dead extern bridge calls outside the live helper with a named diagnostic.
runtime_trap_path rejects missing request anchors, wrong request sentinels, and out-of-order live path construction.
execution_proof rejects dead Odin mutation snippets outside the live heartbeat branch with a named diagnostic.
execution_proof rejects missing nil guards, missing status_bits mutation, out-of-order mutations, and missing serial observation strings.
scripts/verify.sh regenerated latest_verify.json after the hardened validator commits.
validator_coverage reports pass only when every registered validator has focused tests with behavioral negative-path coverage.
A placeholder test containing only a validator token and negative-looking name fails validator_coverage with a named missing-invocation diagnostic.
validator_coverage reports pass only when each declared negative marker maps to an existing behavioral negative test.
Missing marker metadata, missing required markers, unknown markers, missing mapped test functions, and mapped placeholder tests fail with named diagnostics.
K_SYSCALL_NOP is represented in syscall table contract v0 as a no-payload entry that requires abi.K_SYSCALL_NOP, returns K_OK, and rejects payload mutation or heartbeat payload layout usage.
The Rust NOP probe calls syscall_entry with abi::K_SYSCALL_NOP and core::ptr::null_mut(), validates abi::K_OK, and is exercised before the heartbeat request.

syscall_class_contract validates no_payload_status and payload_mutating_status semantics, requires table entries to declare classes, rejects kind/class drift, forbids request/response metadata on no-payload entries, and rejects missing payload-mutating metadata.

K_SYSCALL_STATUS is represented in the ABI manifest, syscall table contract, syscall class contract, live Odin dispatcher, and Rust core service probe as a no-payload status syscall that returns K_OK and does not inspect or mutate payload state.
syscall_catalog validates the cataloged syscall summaries against the ABI manifest, syscall table contract, syscall class contract, registered proof validators, and live Rust runtime probe presence.

syscall_surface_report validates that docs/generated/syscall_surface.md matches the deterministic renderer output and fails on stale content, manual edits, missing syscall sections, missing class summaries, and missing source references.

abi_surface_report validates that docs/generated/abi_surface.md matches the deterministic renderer output and fails on stale content, manual edits, missing constants, missing binding paths, missing layout details, missing sentinels, and manifest-driven drift.

Governance documents now define document precedence, non-negotiable invariants, code construction rules, contract authority, validation policy, generated artifact policy, compatibility boundaries, security boundary rules, and ADR requirements while keeping architecture diagrams descriptive.
rust-toolchain.toml pins Rust 1.96.0 and scripts/verify.sh resolves and invokes cargo and rustc from the pinned toolchain directly.

governance_index_report validates that docs/generated/governance_index.md matches the deterministic renderer output for active contracts, schemas, validators, generated reports, latest verification artifact, current version, and non-goals.
harness/validators_impl/abi.py remains a documented compatibility shim for the registered abi validator while unused ABI generator helpers are pruned.

docs/PHASEMAP.md, docs/ROADMAP.md, and docs/RELEASE_EVIDENCE.md define release sequencing, direction, gates, evidence, and blocker categories without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, or compatibility claims.

docs/RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md and docs/REQUIRED_CHECKS.md define release approval and required-check policy while docs/RELEASE_EVIDENCE.md, docs/PHASEMAP.md, and docs/ROADMAP.md link to those policies without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, or compatibility claims.

scripts/runtime_smoke.sh generates artifacts/runtime/runtime_smoke.log from freestanding kernel and assembly object evidence, and runtime_smoke_evidence validates the artifact while explicitly documenting that it is not QEMU boot or hardware trap evidence.

scripts/runtime_smoke.sh generates deterministic artifacts/runtime/runtime_smoke.metadata.json, runtime_smoke_evidence validates metadata claims and non-goals, and release evidence docs define live and packaged runtime evidence paths.

docs/DOCUMENTATION_STANDARD.md defines documentation construction and review standards while docs/GOVERNANCE.md records its authority boundary below higher technical governance documents and above descriptive documents.

docs/CODING_STYLE.md now points to docs/GOVERNANCE.md for the complete precedence order, and docs/GENERATED_ARTIFACTS.md names harness/governance_index_report.py and governance_index_report for docs/generated/governance_index.md.

runtime_evidence_review validates docs/RUNTIME_EVIDENCE_REVIEW.md, release evidence/checklist/check policy references, and runtime smoke metadata non-goals while keeping runtime evidence scoped to runtime-adjacent-object-symbol-smoke.

Full CI runs scripts/verify.sh, uploads artifacts/runtime/runtime_smoke.log and artifacts/runtime/runtime_smoke.metadata.json, lint remains static-check only, and release review requires runtime smoke evidence without changing runtime behavior or evidence scope.

v0.3.0 boot feasibility is blocked by missing_boot_protocol_and_image_packaging; scripts/boot_blocker_report.sh generates artifacts/runtime/boot_blocker_report.json and boot_blocker_report validates the report and documentation references.

v0.3.1 selects Limine as the initial x86_64 boot protocol for a future QEMU serial smoke path while preserving the active boot blocker and avoiding QEMU boot, hardware trap, compatibility, userspace, subsystem, or production-readiness claims.
v0.3.2 stages a Limine boot image skeleton and kernel ELF build path under artifacts/runtime/boot_image while boot remains blocked on missing QEMU smoke execution and serial evidence capture.

v0.3.3 adds scripts/qemu_smoke.sh and records the narrower missing_bootable_iso_packaging blocker because the repository stages an image root and kernel ELF but does not yet produce a bootable Limine ISO or disk image.

v0.3.4 generates boot image package metadata and records missing_bootable_iso_generation because the repository still lacks Limine ISO packaging commands, bootloader installation artifacts, and an xorriso-compatible ISO builder.

v0.3.5 documents Limine and xorriso acquisition, local and CI install paths, provenance rules, and narrows the active blocker to missing_bootable_iso_generation without adding a bootable ISO, QEMU boot, serial marker, hardware trap, compatibility, userspace, subsystem, or production-readiness claims.

v0.3.6 implements the Limine/xorriso ISO generation path in scripts/build_boot_image.sh and records missing_iso_generation_tooling because the local environment lacks Limine executables, Limine bootloader artifacts, and xorriso; no bootable ISO, QEMU boot, serial marker, hardware trap, compatibility, userspace, subsystem, or production-readiness claim is added.

v0.3.7 installs xorriso and pinned Limine v12.3.3 source tooling in full CI, runs scripts/build_boot_image.sh before full verification, uploads boot image metadata and any generated ISO, and preserves no-QEMU-boot, no-serial-success, hardware-trap, compatibility, userspace, subsystem, and production-readiness non-claims.

v0.3.8 adds QEMU smoke metadata, qemu_smoke_evidence validation, a bounded QEMU execution path, CI QEMU smoke artifact upload, and the kernel-emitted KOZO_BOOT_SMOKE_OK marker; local evidence remains blocked by missing_iso_generation_tooling unless ISO tooling or a packaged ISO is available.

v0.3.9 records the CI-observed qemu_timeout blocker, adds artifacts/runtime/qemu_smoke.stderr.log evidence, and preserves the no-QEMU-boot claim unless KOZO_BOOT_SMOKE_OK appears in captured serial output.

v0.4.0 adds Limine serial/verbose config, KOZO early marker instrumentation, diagnostic QEMU metadata, and narrowed reachability blockers while preserving no-QEMU-boot and non-compatibility claim boundaries.
v0.4.1 updates the Limine kernel path and QEMU smoke taxonomy; full verification must refresh local blocked metadata separately from source changes

v0.4.2 adds deterministic kernel ELF loadability reporting, validates the staged kernel ELF structure, and adds ISO filename metadata for Limine path visibility while preserving no-QEMU-boot, no-kernel-entry, no-serial-init, and non-compatibility claim boundaries

v0.4.3 adds a host dependency portability gate, removes local Homebrew-style Limine fallback paths, documents CI/Linux as authoritative portability proof, and preserves local macOS as convenience-only without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, QEMU boot, compatibility, or production-readiness claims

v0.4.4 changes Limine config to boot():/boot/kozo/kozo-kernel.elf, records configured and normalized kernel path metadata plus ISO contents visibility, and preserves no-QEMU-boot, no-kernel-entry, no-serial-init, compatibility, and production-readiness non-claims
Moved release planning documents under docs/, updated active release evidence/checklist and boot protocol validator references, and preserved runtime and compatibility non-goals

v0.4.5 classifies Limine lower-half PHDR rejection as limine_lower_half_phdr in kernel ELF reports, QEMU smoke metadata, boot blocker reporting, focused tests, and boot evidence docs while preserving no-QEMU-boot and non-compatibility claim boundaries
v0.4.6 adds docs/CODEBASE_AUDIT.md, updates phase and roadmap sequencing so v0.4.7 is the higher-half linker/entry transition, and records that no cleanup was applied during the audit phase

v0.4.95 adds a code quality and style audit section before v0.5.0, updates phase and roadmap sequencing so v0.5.0 is Fix Boot Smoke Marker Emission, and records that no safe cleanup was applied during the audit phase

v0.4.96 adds a non-authoritative QEMU smoke summary artifact, validates that it agrees with metadata and references the underlying logs, uploads it from CI, and keeps v0.5.0 focused on Fix Boot Smoke Marker Emission
v0.5.0 emits KOZO_BOOT_SMOKE_OK from _start through the proven COM1 path and hardens qemu_smoke_evidence so pass metadata requires the full ordered marker sequence

v0.5.1 records ci_verification_failed_after_v0.5.0 as the active release blocker, keeps missing_iso_generation_tooling as the local generated blocker, removes stale ABI/syscall maturity next-step wording, and preserves all no-QEMU-boot, compatibility, and production-readiness claim boundaries.

CI evidence access hardening adds scripts/ci_evidence_summary.sh and full-CI log summary output without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, linker layout, QEMU marker semantics, compatibility claims, or production-readiness claims.
runtime_halt_contract validates that boot.asm emits KOZO_BOOT_SMOKE_OK before entering a cli/hlt loop with structural fallthrough forbidden.

runtime_progression_contract validates halt-preservation prerequisites, contract references, transition requirements, forbidden shortcuts, evidence requirements, and non-goals without defining stage order.

runtime_progression_entry_contract validates current contract references, progression marker reservation, prerequisites, evidence requirements, transition requirements, forbidden shortcuts, its memory-to-entry proof boundary, and non-goals without defining stage order.
v0.6.4 removes two zero-byte unreferenced generator stubs, documents oversized/dead/stale/shim findings, and preserves validator outcomes through compileall, unit discovery, and full verification.

v0.6.5 adds runtime_evidence_taxonomy contract, schema, loader, validator, and focused tests; QEMU smoke and boot blocker validators consume taxonomy helpers while marker strings, marker order, and pass criteria remain unchanged.

Added runtime_progression_stages contract, schema, loader, validator, focused tests, registry/schema wiring, planning/evidence documentation, audit alignment, changelog entry, and refreshed generated governance/proof state.

Added stack_initialization_evidence_contract as the planning authority for future STACK_INITIALIZATION_EVIDENCE, reserved KOZO_STACK_INIT_OK without emitting it, aligned progression contracts, and preserved runtime behavior.

v0.7.0 implements controlled boot stack evidence by setting rsp to boot_stack_top, probing stack use, emitting KOZO_STACK_INIT_OK, extending the governed marker sequence, and preserving no memory/runtime/userspace/compatibility/production claims.

v0.7.2 orders BOOT_SMOKE, STACK_INITIALIZATION_EVIDENCE, MEMORY_INITIALIZATION_EVIDENCE, and RUNTIME_PROGRESSION_ENTRY monotonically, leaves stack proven and later stages planned, and validates graph and authority integrity.

v0.7.4 implements the contract-owned 4096-byte static memory region, explicit zero fill, bounded sentinel write/read/compare/restore probe, KOZO_MEMORY_INIT_OK marker, and unchanged terminal halt loop.

v0.7.45 is accepted by hosted CI marker and validator evidence for the bounded assembly-to-Odin call, fixed bootstrap context validation, volatile static-state probe, exact return status, and preserved terminal halt.

explanation_summary: Made scripts/verify.sh reproducible on clean trees, added fresh build/check evidence generation, and emitted the generated artifact JSON directly.
Replaced the broken default serial inline assembly path with a build-safe stub so kernel builds and object generation succeed consistently.
Replaced the Rust heartbeat stub with an extern syscall_entry bridge call, added runtime_trap_path validation, and aligned the repository status with the exercised assembly bridge boundary.
Locked the canonical runtime-trap wording into README.md and ADR-0016 and ignored generated __pycache__/ directories to keep the workspace clean.
Removed the tracked Python cache artifacts from version control so ignored cache files no longer pollute repository state.
Added return_path_proof so the Rust caller must explicitly validate the returned payload mutations after the bridge call, with negative proofs for wrong sequence and missing timestamp checks.
Hardened return_path_proof so status_bits checks and writes are required anchors with focused negative unit coverage.
Hardened bridge_alignment so ordered live syscall_entry anchors are required and covered by focused negative tests.
Hardened runtime_trap_path so the live Rust heartbeat request path and bridge helper are required and covered by focused negative tests.
Hardened execution_proof so the live Odin heartbeat branch and stable serial observations are required and covered by focused negative tests.
Regenerated latest_verify.json so the current artifact records the hardened proof details.
Added validator_coverage so registered validators must have focused test files with AST-verified behavioral negative-path coverage.
Extended validator_coverage so registered validators must declare marker-level coverage for their known false-pass risks.
Promoted K_SYSCALL_NOP into a governed no-payload syscall table entry without adding payload layout, userspace execution, Linux compatibility, or broader runtime behavior.
Added a narrow Rust NOP probe through the existing syscall_entry bridge using a null payload argument and no payload mutation.
Added syscall class contract v0 so currently proven syscall table entries declare semantic classes without adding new syscalls or runtime behavior.

Added K_SYSCALL_STATUS as a governed no-payload status syscall without adding Linux compatibility, userspace generalization, process model behavior, VFS behavior, scheduler behavior, ELF loading, file descriptor behavior, or runtime subsystem behavior.
Added syscall catalog v0 as a governed summary of existing syscall contracts without adding new syscalls or runtime behavior.
Added generated syscall surface report as non-authoritative Markdown derived from existing contracts without adding new syscalls or runtime behavior.
Added generated ABI surface report as non-authoritative Markdown derived from the ABI manifest without changing ABI constants, ABI layouts, generated bindings, syscalls, or runtime behavior.
Added governance document authority split without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall contracts, generated bindings, validator logic, or compatibility behavior.
Pinned Rust verification to cargo and rustc 1.96.0 without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall contracts, generated bindings, or validator logic.
Updated verification and CI to select the Rust toolchain declared by rust-toolchain.toml and invoke its cargo and rustc binaries directly instead of relying on ambient cargo or host stable dispatch.

Added generated governance index as a non-authoritative report over active governance surfaces without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall contracts, generated bindings, or compatibility behavior.
Documented the ABI validator shim and pruned unused ABI generator helpers without changing generated bindings, ABI contracts, runtime behavior, or validator behavior.
Added release planning documents for KOZO v1.0.0 without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, or compatibility claims.
Added release checklist and required checks policy for v0.1.0 release governance baseline execution without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, or compatibility claims.

Added a bounded runtime smoke evidence baseline for v0.2.0 using freestanding object and symbol evidence without adding runtime features, ABI changes, syscall changes, compatibility claims, or production-readiness claims.

Added runtime evidence packaging metadata and release bundle guidance for v0.2.1 without adding runtime features, ABI changes, syscall changes, QEMU boot claims, compatibility claims, or production-readiness claims.

Added documentation clarity governance standard without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, generated bindings, validator logic, compatibility claims, or production-readiness claims.

Fixed documentation authority consistency issues by removing a duplicated precedence list from coding style guidance and replacing governance-index generated artifact placeholder wording with actual generator and validator references.

Added runtime evidence review gate for release claim discipline without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, QEMU boot evidence, hardware trap evidence, compatibility claims, userspace execution claims, or production-readiness claims.

Aligned CI/runtime evidence policy so full CI requires and uploads runtime smoke evidence through scripts/verify.sh, lint remains static unless it runs full verification, and release evidence/checklist docs require runtime smoke log and metadata without expanding runtime claims.

Added the v0.3.0 bootable runtime baseline blocker path: boot is not yet honestly claimable, the missing boot protocol/linker/loader/image packaging components are reported and validated, and release evidence includes the blocker artifact without changing runtime, ABI, or syscall behavior.

Selected Limine as the initial x86_64 boot protocol, added an accepted ADR and boot protocol plan, registered boot_protocol_decision validation, and kept the v0.3.0 blocker active until implementation adds linker, loader, image packaging, and QEMU smoke evidence.

Added the v0.3.2 boot image skeleton with linker script, Limine config, boot image staging script, freestanding memory support, skeleton validation, and reduced blocker state while preserving the no-QEMU-boot claim boundary.

Added a fail-closed v0.3.3 QEMU smoke command and refined the active blocker to missing_bootable_iso_packaging without adding QEMU boot, serial marker, hardware trap, compatibility, userspace, subsystem, or production-readiness claims.

Added v0.3.4 boot image packaging metadata and boot_image_packaging validation, narrowing the active blocker to missing_bootable_iso_generation without adding a bootable ISO, QEMU boot, serial marker, hardware trap, compatibility, userspace, subsystem, or production-readiness claims.

Added v0.3.5 boot tooling acquisition policy and boot_tooling validation, narrowing the blocker from missing_limine_iso_tooling to missing_bootable_iso_generation while preserving all no-boot and non-compatibility claim boundaries.

Added v0.3.6 ISO generation command path and updated packaging validation to distinguish real ISO generation from a blocked missing_iso_generation_tooling result while preserving all no-boot and non-compatibility claim boundaries.

Added v0.3.7 CI ISO tooling install with pinned Limine source acquisition, xorriso installation, build-script env hooks, CI boot image build attempts, artifact upload coverage, and boot_tooling validation while preserving all no-boot and non-compatibility claim boundaries.

Added v0.3.8 QEMU serial smoke evidence gate with deterministic pass/blocked metadata, qemu_smoke_evidence validation, CI QEMU smoke attempts, and a kernel-emitted boot marker while preserving no-boot and non-compatibility claim boundaries.

Hardened v0.3.9 QEMU timeout blocker handling with stderr evidence, phase-aligned boot blocker reporting, post-packaging exact QEMU blockers, and marker-present blocked-metadata rejection while preserving no-boot and non-compatibility claim boundaries.

Added v0.4.0 kernel entry reachability diagnostics so QEMU smoke can distinguish Limine reachability, kernel load, kernel entry, serial initialization, marker emission, and fallback timeout without claiming QEMU boot.
Added v0.4.1 Limine kernel load path fix so the configured kernel path matches the staged ISO layout and Limine open failures no longer masquerade as kernel entry failures.

Added v0.4.2 kernel ELF loadability evidence so kernel_not_loaded can be separated from invalid ELF shape, missing PT_LOAD segments, invalid entry point, or linker output failures without claiming QEMU boot.

Added v0.4.3 host dependency portability gate so build, verification, ISO, ELF, and QEMU smoke tooling depend on declared CI/Linux tooling, controlled environment variables, command discovery, or repository-relative paths instead of local host state.
Added v0.4.4 Limine ISO/kernel load semantics so the configured boot(): path, normalized ISO path, and ISO contents visibility are recorded and validated without claiming QEMU boot or kernel entry.
Moved release planning documents under docs/ and updated active references so governance planning documents live with the rest of the governance document set.

Added v0.4.5 Limine ELF load layout classification so Limine lower-half PHDR rejection narrows to limine_lower_half_phdr without changing linker layout, runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, or compatibility claims.

Added v0.4.6 codebase structural audit documentation before the higher-half linker/entry transition, documenting duplicated boot taxonomy, stale release/check blocker wording, and transition coupling risks without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, linker, or compatibility behavior.

Added v0.4.7 higher-half linker transition so the kernel ELF records higher-half PT_LOAD virtual addresses with low physical load addresses, clears the local limine_lower_half_phdr loadability report, and preserves no-QEMU-boot, no-kernel-entry, ABI, syscall, and compatibility claim boundaries until CI QEMU evidence advances.

Added v0.4.8 kernel entry handoff instrumentation so _start emits KOZO_EARLY_0_ENTRY through assembly COM1 output before stack setup or Odin code, QEMU smoke metadata records entry handoff fields, and validators reject stale kernel_entry_not_reached classifications once the entry marker appears.

Added v0.4.9 early serial initialization instrumentation so _start emits KOZO_EARLY_1_SERIAL_INIT_START and KOZO_EARLY_2_SERIAL_INIT_OK before stack setup or Odin code, while QEMU smoke validation advances serial-init OK evidence to marker_not_emitted unless the final boot smoke marker appears.

Added v0.4.95 code quality and style audit documentation before v0.5.0, documenting duplicated boot marker taxonomy, duplicated blocker taxonomy, QEMU script and validator coverage size, fixture duplication, and marker-emission risks without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, linker, QEMU marker semantics, or compatibility behavior.

Added v0.4.96 QEMU smoke summary observability so CI uploads a concise, non-authoritative summary derived from existing smoke metadata and logs without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, linker, QEMU marker semantics, or compatibility behavior.
Added v0.5.0 boot smoke marker emission from the proven assembly serial path, with ordered-marker QEMU smoke validation and no ABI, syscall, compatibility, or production-readiness claim changes.

Added v0.5.1 governance planning alignment so PHASEMAP, ROADMAP, boot blockers, runtime evidence, release evidence, audit findings, changelog, and task state reflect the failed pushed v0.5.0 CI verification boundary without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, linker, QEMU smoke, compatibility, or production-readiness claims.
Added v0.5.2 CI evidence access hardening so GitHub Actions logs expose first-level smoke evidence even when artifact download, API log access, or local gh tooling is unavailable.
v0.5.4 promotes CI-proven QEMU serial smoke evidence, accepts pass/none boot blocker state in stale validators, and preserves all no-compatibility and no-production claim boundaries.

v0.7.0 adds stack initialization evidence through the assembly boot path and validator coverage while preserving ABI, syscall, linker, memory, Odin runtime, userspace, compatibility, and production-readiness boundaries.

v0.7.1 adds memory initialization evidence planning through a contract, schema, loader, validator, tests, and progression-stage alignment while preserving runtime behavior, ABI, syscall, linker, QEMU smoke, halt, stack evidence, compatibility, and production-readiness boundaries.
v0.7.2 reconciles the progression model into an acyclic, monotonic, single-owner graph and aligns contracts, tests, planning documents, audit state, and task identity without runtime changes.

v0.7.3 hardens the memory evidence boundary into an implementation-ready static-region specification without runtime, marker, halt, ABI, syscall, linker, compatibility, or production-readiness changes.

v0.7.4 implements only the governed static-region memory evidence path, adds structural and captured-evidence validation, preserves the halt loop, and does not add paging, allocation, Odin runtime execution, userspace, compatibility, or production-readiness claims.

v0.7.45 adds a governed internal assembly-to-Odin progression boundary, bounded Odin state evidence, exact return handling, linked-symbol reporting, and progression evidence validation while preserving terminal halt authority and all subsystem, compatibility, and production non-goals.

v0.7.5 adds a contract-backed three-iteration Odin loop with volatile state mutation, ordered marker bridges, deterministic status handling, ELF backward-edge reporting, the unchanged exact return-to-halt boundary, and hosted CI acceptance.

v0.8.0 adds one explicit internal runtime status request, fixed response, direct dispatcher, bounded handler, source and ELF evidence validation, fixed capability markers, and the unchanged exact return-to-halt boundary while leaving hosted capability evidence pending.
